title: Closures created in a loop capture the loop variable by reference, not its value
rule: In Python (and JavaScript declared with var), a closure defined inside a loop captures the loop variable itself, not its value at the moment of creation, so after the loop every closure sees the variable's final value. Bind the current value explicitly: a default argument (lambda i=i: ...) in Python, or let/const per iteration in JavaScript.
triggers: all closures created in a loop return the same last value
a list of lambdas built in a loop all print the final index
callbacks registered in a for loop all use the last iteration's value
function defined inside a loop captures the wrong variable value
